# sim.Simulation { #tspaint.sim.Simulation }

```python
tspaint.sim.Simulation(
    ts,
    queries,
    labels,
    truth_states,
    sample_sets,
    ghost_states=None,
    source_states=(),
    demography=None,
)
```

A simulated admixture with known truth — the return of :func:`simulate_admixture`.

Everything downstream needs (queries to paint, reference labels, ground-truth ancestry) is
derived from the demography's own population **roles** (see :func:`admixture_demography`), so no
caller ever needs the population-name constants.

## Attributes {.doc-section .doc-section-attributes}

<code>[**ts**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[tskit](`tskit`).[TreeSequence](`tskit.TreeSequence`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The simulated tree sequence — **with mutations** when ``mutation_rate`` was given.

<code>[**queries**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[list](`list`)\[[int](`int`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Query (admixed) haplotype node ids.

<code>[**labels**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [int](`int`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Default reference panel: reference haplotype node id → ancestry-state index (``0`` / ``1``), ready to pass to :func:`tspaint.paint`.

<code>[**truth_states**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [list](`list`)\[[tuple](`tuple`)\[[float](`float`), [float](`float`), [int](`int`)\]\]\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Per query, the true local-ancestry tracts ``(left, right, state)`` from the census — ready to score against a painting (e.g. :func:`tspaint.validate.balanced_accuracy`). **Ghost tracts are embedded here** with their own state index (beyond the source states), so they appear in the truth band of :meth:`tspaint.Painting.plot`\ 's truth track alongside the source segments.

<code>[**sample_sets**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[str](`str`), [list](`list`)\[[int](`int`)\]\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Population name → its sample node ids, for building custom reference panels (e.g. proxy-only vs direct-source labels).

<code>[**ghost_states**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [list](`list`)\[[tuple](`tuple`)\[[float](`float`), [float](`float`), [int](`int`)\]\]\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Per query, the ghost-only subset of :attr:`truth_states` — tracts descending from a ``ghost``-tagged (unsampled, un-paintable) source. Empty when the demography has no ghost. The ground truth to score a reference-free :func:`tspaint.detect_ghost` against.

<code>[**source_states**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[tuple](`tuple`)\[[int](`int`), ...\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The **paintable** ancestry-state indices (from ``source``/``reference`` populations), sorted — e.g. ``(0, 1, 2)`` for three sources. Pass ``K=len(source_states)`` to :func:`tspaint.paint`; any state in :attr:`truth_states` beyond these is a ghost.

<code>[**demography**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[msprime](`msprime`).[Demography](`msprime.Demography`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The role-tagged demography the tree sequence was simulated under (the ``demography`` argument to :func:`simulate_admixture`), retained so the true split / admixture times and population structure are recoverable from the result (e.g. to check a dated split against the truth).
